concepts.title: Gamma function
concepts.conceptBody:
Gamma Function 

It is defined by the improper integral, by $\int _{0}^{\infty }{{\text{e}}^{-\text{x}}}{{\text{x}}^{\text{n}-1}}\text{dx}$ and is denoted by $\Gamma \text{ n}$

$\therefore \Gamma\text{ n}=\mathop{\int }_{0}^{\infty }{{\text{e}}^{-\text{x}}}{{\text{x}}^{\text{n}-1}}\text{dx }\text{ where }\text{ x }\text{ is }\text{ a }\text{ positive }\text{ rational }\text{ number}\text{. }$

Properties of Gamma function

$\begin{array}{l} \text{1}\text{.} & \Gamma1=1,\Gamma0=\infty \text{ and }\Gamma\left( n+1 \right)=n\Gamma \text{ n} \\ \text{2}\text{.} & \text{ if }n\in N,\Gamma\left( n+1 \right)=n! \\ \text{3}\text{. } & \Gamma\left( 1/2 \right)=\sqrt{\pi } \\ \end{array}$

Useful extensions of gamma function:

  1. $\int _{0}^{\pi /2}\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{2}}xdx=\int _{0}^{\pi /2}\text{co}{{\text{s}}^{n}}xdx$

\(=\left\{ \begin{array}{*{35}{l}} \frac{n-1}{n}\cdot \frac{n-3}{n-2}\cdot \frac{n-5}{n-4}\cdots \frac{3}{4}\cdot \frac{1}{2}\left( \frac{\pi }{2}. \right) &  \text{ if } n \text{ is }\text{ even }  \\ \frac{n-1}{n}\cdot \frac{n-3}{n-2}\cdot \frac{n-5}{n-4}\cdots \frac{4}{5}\cdot \frac{2}{3}\cdot 1, &  \text{ if } n \text{ is }\text{ odd }  \\ \end{array} \right.\)

  1. $\int _{0}^{\pi /2}\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{\text{m}}}x\text{co}{{\text{s}}^{\text{n}}}\text{xdx}=\int _{0}^{\pi /2}\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{\text{n}}}\text{xco}{{\text{s}}^{\text{m}}}\text{xdx}$

$\begin{array}{l} {} & =\frac{\left( m-1 \right)\left( m-3 \right)\ldots \left( 1 \right)\left( n-1 \right)\left( n-3 \right)\ldots \left( 1 \right)}{\left( m+n \right)\left( m+n-2 \right)\ldots 2}\cdot \frac{\pi }{2}  \text{ when }\text{ both } m~\And~ n \text{ belong }\text{ to }\text{ even }\text{ integer }  \\ {} & =\frac{\left( m-1 \right)\left( m-3 \right)\ldots \left( 1 \text{ or } 2 \right)\left( n-1 \right)\left( n-3 \right)\ldots \left( 1 \text{ or } 2 \right)}{\left( m+n \right)\left( m+n-2 \right)\ldots 1 \text{ or } 2} \text{ when }\text{ either }\text{ of } m \text{ or } n \text{ belong }\text{ to }\text{ odd }\text{ integer }  \\ \end{array}$

  1. $\int _{0}^{\pi }\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{\text{m}}}x\text{co}{{\text{s}}^{\text{n}}}\text{xdx}=0,$ if $\text{n}$ is odd

$=2\mathop{\int }_{0}^{\pi /2}\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{\text{m}}}\text{xco}{{\text{s}}^{\text{n}}}\text{xdx},\text{ if }\text{ n }\text{ is }\text{ even }$

examples.title: The correct evaluation of $\int_0^\pi\left|\sin ^4 x\right| d x$ is
$\int_{-\pi / 2}^{\pi / 2} \sin ^2 x \cos ^2 x(\sin x+\cos x) d x=$
